compiled_state.title: As a developer, I can run the scaffold-route recipe to generate framework-specific route boilerplate
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Scaffold-route recipe for Python/Flask and TypeScript/NextJS — generates framework-specific route boilerplate files.
**Why**: Demonstrates the recipe system handles generative recipes (creating new files) not just transforms (modifying existing files). This validates the registry schema extensibility and executor rollback for file creation.
**Scope**:
- IN: scaffold-route recipe for Flask and NextJS, generative recipe support in executor (file creation rollback), engine adapter per S1's documented interface
- OUT: Ruby scaffold-route, add-parameter (S1/S2), normalize-imports (S4)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a developer can run scaffold-route with a route name and it generates correct Flask route boilerplate (blueprint, view function, template) and NextJS route boilerplate (page, API route)
  ← Satisfies: "Initial catalog includes scaffold-route (framework-specific boilerplate)"
- When this story is complete, running scaffold-route twice produces identical output — re-running when files already exist either skips or overwrites idempotently (no duplicated content)
  ← Satisfies: "Each recipe is idempotent"
- When this story is complete, 3 consecutive runs produce zero-variance output
  ← Satisfies: "Determinism: 3 consecutive runs produce zero-variance output"
- When this story is complete, executor rollback correctly handles failed scaffold-route (deletes created files rather than stash-popping)
  ← Satisfies: executor handles generative recipe rollback (adversarial review finding)
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Generative recipe idempotency semantics differ from transform recipes — must define behavior when target files already exist (skip vs overwrite). Document the chosen semantics in the recipe registry entry.
- [Reliability] Executor rollback for file creation requires file deletion, not git stash pop — the executor must distinguish between transform and generative rollback paths based on the recipe type in the registry.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
